---
title: "About this project"
subtitle: "Reproducibility, data protection, and AI use"
date: today
toc: true
---

This page collects the statements that belong with any shared analysis. Adapt
the wording to your own project before you publish.

## Reproducibility

All analyses were conducted in R using a structured project. Data preparation,
models, figures, and tables are generated by the reports in `reports/` from the
shared functions in `R/`; the R environment is pinned with
[`renv`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/). Anyone can reproduce this site from a
clean clone:

```bash
R -e 'renv::restore()'
quarto render reports/webpage
```

Because every page reads the same cleaned dataset and calls the same functions,
changing a model in one place updates this website **and** the journal
manuscript on the next render. Nothing is copy-pasted between them.

## Data protection

This project ships **simulated teaching data** (`data/mock/`). No real
participant data is included in the repository. Real data, if used, lives in
`data/raw/` and `data/processed/`, which are gitignored and never committed —
so the analysis is fully shareable while the underlying data stays private.
Output tables and figures are checked for identifiability before sharing.

> Scripts can usually be shared. Raw data usually cannot. Outputs depend on
> identifiability.

## AI use

AI tools were used to support code explanation, debugging, documentation, and
drafting of non-final text. No confidential or identifiable participant data
was sent to external AI systems, and all AI-assisted code and text were
reviewed by the authors before use. Editor-level and tool-level rules
(`the-science-repository.llm.code-workspace` and `.claude/settings.json`) keep
the assistant from reading `data/raw/` and `data/processed/`.

*Keep only the sentences that are true for your project.*
